title: Post-Processor Registry Lifecycle
activity_flow: Read config key CUSTOM_POST_PROCESSORS → comma-separated processor name list
FORK: for each processorName in processorNames → call getPostProcessor(config, name)
[Branch A] Check new convention: config.subset('post.processors.<name>') non-empty?
[A-NO]  Check old convention: config.hasKey('<name>.type') AND config.subset('<name>') non-empty?
[A-NO-MISSING] Throw DebeziumException('Post processor X is missing X.type and/or X.<option>')
JOIN: collect all config-based PostProcessor instances into list
Construct new PostProcessorRegistry(processors) — stores as unmodifiable list
INJECTION PHASE: @InjectService → setBeanRegistry(BeanRegistry) called on registry
INJECTION PHASE: injectServiceRegistry(ServiceRegistry) called on registry
For each PostProcessor: if instanceof BeanRegistryAware → injectBeanRegistry(beanRegistry)
For each PostProcessor: if instanceof ServiceRegistryAware → injectServiceRegistry(serviceRegistry)
RUNTIME PHASE (external caller): For each CDC event → PostProcessor.apply(Object key, Struct value)

decision_points.condition: Old convention valid? (config.hasKey('<name>.type') AND subset non-empty)
decision_points.yes: Log deprecation warning, use old convention instantiation + configure
Invoke factory.get() and append external processors

key_relationships: PostProcessorRegistry implements Service, Startable, Closeable, ServiceRegistryAware
PostProcessorRegistryServiceProvider uses ServiceLoader<PostProcessorFactory> for SPI discovery
error_handling: DebeziumException thrown when processor name lacks '.type' key OR has empty config subset
Error message: 'Post processor X is missing X.type and/or X.<option> configurations'
important_notes: Old config convention (name.*) is deprecated — WARN logged; new convention is post.processors.<name>.*
Ordering: config-defined processors come before SPI-discovered (external) processors
PostProcessorRegistry is @ThreadSafe; processor list is immutable after construction
Injection (BeanRegistry/ServiceRegistry) happens at start(), not at construction
apply() is NOT called by PostProcessorRegistry itself — caller (connector pipeline) iterates getProcessors() and calls apply() per event
